name: Szerb Bableves (Serbian Bean Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak beans in 1 quart cold water the night before you plan to make soup.
2. Drain beans and set aside.
3. In a large soup pot, cook bones and vegetables with salt over low heat for 2 hours using 2 quarts of water. Strain and save the liquid.
4. Add drained beans to the reserved liquid, adding enough water to bring the total amount back to 2 quarts. Cook covered over low heat until beans are tender.
5. Make little dumplings and set aside.
6. Melt lard in a frying pan. Wilt onion, then mix in flour and cook over very low heat until the mixture is light brown.
7. Remove from heat and add mashed garlic and paprika. Whisk in 1/2 cup cold water. Stir into the almost ready bean soup and cook over very low heat for at least 10 minutes longer.
Note: This southern Hungarian dish may appear in Serbian cookbooks as a Serbian dish, where it is hopefully titled "Hungarian Bean Soup".
